title: Ops runbook + outcome tracking + maintenance rotation + annual re-verification cadence + gated expansion process
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This post-delivery task establishes sustainable operations: an ops runbook, beneficiary-outcome tracking (people helped, not page views), a named maintenance rotation, an annual legal-content re-verification cadence tied to the real update points, and a documented expert-gated process for adding programs, jurisdictions, and languages.
objective: Write the ops runbook and establish outcome tracking, a maintenance rotation, an annual re-verification cadence, and a gated expansion process for sustained operation.
acceptanceCriteria: Runbook covers deploy, content updates, source re-verification, translation re-review, and partner support

Outcome tracking records people helped / navigator time-saved / fewer wrong applications (not page views); annual re-verification cadence tied to poverty-guideline (January) + SNAP COLA (October) update points, enforced by the staleness fail-safe
Named maintenance rotation; documented, expert-gated process for adding programs, jurisdictions, and languages
